articles.title: Understanding FAR Compliance for New Contractors
articles.author: Kurozato Team
articles.excerpt: A comprehensive guide to Federal Acquisition Regulation compliance for companies entering the federal marketplace.
articles.content:
# Understanding FAR Compliance

The Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) can seem overwhelming for companies new to federal contracting. This guide will help you understand the basics.

## What is FAR?

The FAR is the principal set of rules governing federal government procurement. It establishes uniform policies for acquisition of supplies and services.

## Key FAR Requirements

- Cost accounting standards
- Labor standards and wage requirements
- Intellectual property rights
- Contract administration procedures
- Dispute resolution processes

## How Kurozato Helps

As your Federal Prime Integrator, we manage all FAR compliance requirements, allowing you to focus on technical delivery.

## Getting Started

The first step is understanding your NAICS codes and ensuring proper SAM.gov registration. Contact us to learn more about our integration services.

articles.title: DCAA Audit Readiness: A Primer
articles.author: Kurozato Team
articles.excerpt: Learn what it takes to maintain audit-ready documentation and pass DCAA reviews with confidence.
articles.content:
# DCAA Audit Readiness

The Defense Contract Audit Agency (DCAA) sets strict standards for accounting systems used by government contractors.

## Why DCAA Compliance Matters

Without proper DCAA-compliant accounting, you cannot:

- Bill the government for your work
- Receive payment on time
- Maintain your contractor status

## Key Requirements

- Timekeeping systems with daily entries
- Job cost accounting
- Indirect cost pools
- Approved accounting system

## The Kurozato Advantage

We maintain **audit-ready documentation** from day one, ensuring you're always prepared for DCAA reviews.

## Common Pitfalls

Many contractors fail audits due to:

- Inadequate timekeeping records
- Mixing personal and business expenses
- Lack of supporting documentation
- Inconsistent accounting methods

## Next Steps

Partner with Kurozato to ensure your accounting systems meet DCAA standards before your first audit.

articles.title: The Hidden Costs of Going Direct
articles.author: Kurozato Team
articles.excerpt: Why attempting to navigate federal contracting alone often costs more than partnering with an integrator.
articles.content:
# The Hidden Costs of Going Direct

Many commercial firms consider pursuing federal contracts directly, believing it will save money. The reality is often quite different.

## The True Cost of DIY Federal Contracting

### Consultant Fees

Most firms hire multiple consultants:

- FAR compliance expert: $200-400/hour
- DCAA accounting specialist: $150-300/hour
- Proposal writer: $100-200/hour
- Registration specialist: $2,000-5,000 flat fee

### Internal Staff Time

Your team spends countless hours:

- Learning federal procurement rules
- Attending training sessions
- Managing compliance documentation
- Responding to audits

### Opportunity Costs

While focused on compliance, you're not:

- Developing new products
- Serving existing customers
- Growing your commercial business

## The Integrator Alternative

Kurozato provides all these services under one roof, allowing you to focus on **technical excellence** while we handle the bureaucracy.

## Cost Comparison

A typical small business pursuing a $500K federal contract:

**DIY Approach:**
- Consultants: $50,000
- Internal time: $30,000
- Failed proposals: $20,000
- **Total: $100,000**

**Kurozato Integration:**
- Management fee: 8-12% of contract value
- Zero internal overhead
- Audit-ready from day one

## Ready to Compare?

Contact us for a detailed cost analysis specific to your situation.
